improving at avoidance is unironically like improving at bms except less streamlined
but the general advice is don't hug the corner, don't jump too much, and look ahead of the kid instead of at the kid

i remember watching a high level smw player get into fangames
"see" ran straight into a cherry moving at 1px per frame
and like, they really struggled with the idea of varying jump heights
it does but i think its less relevant than in fangames
there are 23 different jump heights if we don't count cactuses
and that's just the first jump
yeah, there's a lot of options in fangames
as opposed to like "jump low, mid, or high"
its that one plant in the desert that hurts to touch
basically every jump height in fangame is relevant
full jumps solve most problems in Mario games, and low jumps solve the few things that full jumps can't
the most basic jump having 2 frames of leniency is kind of unhinged
the only other game ive seen where people talk about jump heights like fangames is smb1 speedrunning
meanwhile in fangames "you have two frames of leniency to stop jumping, good luck"
where they talk about like 5 frame jumps and stuff sometimes for setups

ye u shouldve gone under the bouncing cherry
honestly thats evidence of how avoidance feels like ur supposed to be on the left
i guess some addendums to the "dont go in the corner rule"
i wish the twitch character limit was higher so i could post the ttbb wave analogy
its mostly important for stuff like the 2nd attack, continuous attacks where you need to dodge a prolonged pattern where the corner leaves you with few options to dodge
stuff like the orange burst and hearts are just one wave so being away from where they come from to give them time to disperse helps a lot
i don't wanna be super backseaty but i do wanna like help lol
i basically just don't wanna give blanket advice like "dont go in the corner" and not elaborate, but you def get it
i guess one more thing about avoidance is trying to avoid macro clusters if you can parse them
anytime u die to something thats a fully static pattern that just gives u another waypoint for measuring where the safespot is
sorry had to be right back, did you get a pb?
